task: Type-3 DFIG fixed 0.10 ohm fault duration response boundary scan

trend_observations.project_lvrt_reference_boundary_note: Using the previously recorded project convention and a Vmin-based conservative timer reference, 1.25 s and 1.50 s exceed the reference window; this scan did not implement or prove actual tripping.
trend_observations.chopper_semantic_note: BRK_CHOP=1 is active chopper switch command/state; branch current, resistor power, and energy dissipation were not independently measured.
trend_observations.ecap_det_semantic_note: Ecap_Det is confirmed DC-link physical voltage in kV; Edc_pu times 1.45 kV is only a consistency cross-check.
trend_observations.recovery_semantic_note: V_recovery_time_s uses the 0.95-1.05 pu sustained recovery criterion, while V_fault_time_below_0p90_s and the paper LVRT reference retain the 0.90 pu boundary.

semantic_limits: This run does not implement LVRT trip logic and does not operate BRK_DFIG.
S1=1 is treated as the GUI-confirmed crowbar switch command active state; S1=0 means the crowbar switch is not commanded on.
Crowbar current:1..3 are internal crowbar branch currents, but exact phase order and positive direction remain unconfirmed.
I_RS:1..3 and Iconv:1..3 are instantaneous three-phase terminal currents, not RMS values.
DFIG_IFLT_A/B/C_KA are external fault-element currents, not internal converter or crowbar branch currents.
DFIG_DBLK_CMD is not a physical trip state.
The project LVRT timer comparison is recorded only as a future external logic reference.
These scan runs do not prove turbine disconnection because BRK_DFIG was not controlled by LVRT logic.
Ecap_Det is confirmed DC-link physical voltage in kV.
Edc_pu is DC-link per-unit voltage using Vdc_base = 1.45 kV.
Edc_pu times 1.45 kV and Ecap_Det are used as a consistency cross-check; pointwise equality is not required because of sampling/filtering/output timing differences.
BRK_CHOP=1 indicates active chopper switch command/state; physical chopper branch current, resistor power, and energy dissipation were not independently measured in this duration scan.
V_recovery_time_s is the first post-fault time when VIBR1_2 enters and remains within 0.95-1.05 pu through the remaining output window.
V_fault_time_below_0p90_s is the duration within the fault window for which VIBR1_2 is below 0.90 pu.

Crowbar S1/DFIG_CROWBAR_STATE=1 indicates active crowbar switch command; S1=0 indicates the crowbar is not inserted, but this must not be broadened into a claim that no internal protection signal responded.

signal_semantics.Ecap_Det: confirmed DC-link physical voltage in kV
signal_semantics.Edc_pu: confirmed DC-link per-unit voltage using Vdc_base = 1.45 kV
signal_semantics.BRK_CHOP: 1 indicates active chopper switch command/state; physical energy dissipation was not independently measured
signal_semantics.S1: 1 indicates active crowbar switch command; 0 indicates crowbar not inserted
signal_semantics.DFIG_CROWBAR_STATE: alias/summary interpretation of S1 crowbar switch command state
signal_semantics.Crowbar current:1..3: internal crowbar branch currents; unit, positive direction, and exact phase order remain not independently confirmed
signal_semantics.I_RS:1..3: RSC/rotor-side converter terminal instantaneous three-phase currents, not RMS
signal_semantics.Iconv:1..3: GSC/grid-side converter terminal instantaneous three-phase currents, not RMS
signal_semantics.Freq_PLL: Type-3 internal PLL frequency only, not a replacement for SPD30

summary_field_definitions.V_recovery_time_s: first post-fault time when VIBR1_2 enters and remains within 0.95-1.05 pu through the remaining output window
summary_field_definitions.V_recovery_status: recovered means the V_recovery_time_s criterion was satisfied within the output window
summary_field_definitions.V_fault_time_below_0p90_s: duration within the fault window for which VIBR1_2 is below 0.90 pu
summary_field_definitions.Ecap_Det_fault_peak_kV: fault-window peak of confirmed DC-link physical voltage Ecap_Det in kV
summary_field_definitions.Edc_pu_fault_peak: fault-window peak of DC-link per-unit voltage using Vdc_base = 1.45 kV
summary_field_definitions.BRK_CHOP_active_during_fault: true when BRK_CHOP exceeds 0.5 during the fault window, meaning active chopper switch command/state was observed
summary_field_definitions.BRK_CHOP_duration_during_fault_s: duration during the fault window for which BRK_CHOP command/state is active
summary_field_definitions.DFIG_CROWBAR_STATE_active: true when S1/DFIG_CROWBAR_STATE exceeds 0.5, meaning crowbar switch command active

recovery_definition.criterion: first post-fault time when VIBR1_2 enters and remains within 0.95-1.05 pu through the remaining output window

distinct_from.V_fault_time_below_0p90_s: fault-window duration for which VIBR1_2 is below 0.90 pu
distinct_from.paper_lvrt_0p90_boundary: 0.90 pu remains a paper LVRT recovery/reference boundary, not the script recovery-time criterion
